title: salvia dorrii var. pilosa
intro: Salvia dorrii var. pilosa is a variety of the desert blue sage plant, a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, blue to purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. The health benefits of Salvia dorrii var. pilosa include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for topical or internal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
